name: Oversight Ladder
summary: A memory device for the accountability layer: committee, audit, ombudsman, court, inquiry, royal commission, election.
whenToUseIt: Use this when diagnosing failure, scandal, misconduct, or public distrust in institutions.
steps: Define the type of failure: legal, administrative, ethical, or systemic.
Identify the lowest-level scrutiny mechanism that can realistically test it.
Escalate only if the lower mechanism is insufficient or lacks trust.
Track what still needs to happen politically after findings are released.
example: A procurement failure may start with audit and committee scrutiny before any argument for a larger public inquiry appears.
